moduleDescription: To learn about scripting in Nim see NimScript

entries.name: buildOS
entries.description: The OS this build is running on. Can be different from system.hostOS for cross compilations.

entries.name: buildCPU
entries.description: The CPU this build is running on. Can be different from system.hostCPU for cross compilations.

entries.name: getCurrentDir
entries.description: Retrieves the current working directory.

entries.name: paramStr
entries.code: proc paramStr(i: int): string {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Retrieves the i'th command line parameter.

entries.name: paramCount
entries.description: Retrieves the number of command line parameters.

entries.name: switch
entries.code: proc switch(key: string; val = "") {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Sets a Nim compiler command line switch, for example switch("checks", "on").

entries.name: warning
entries.code: proc warning(name: string; val: bool) {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Disables or enables a specific warning.

entries.name: hint
entries.code: proc hint(name: string; val: bool) {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Disables or enables a specific hint.

entries.name: patchFile
entries.code:
proc patchFile(package, filename, replacement: string) {.raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Overrides the location of a given file belonging to the passed package. If the replacement is not an absolute path, the path is interpreted to be local to the Nimscript file that contains the call to patchFile, Nim's --path is not used at all to resolve the filename! The compiler also performs path substitution on replacement.

Example:

patchFile("stdlib", "asyncdispatch", "patches/replacement")

entries.name: getCommand
entries.description: Gets the Nim command that the compiler has been invoked with, for example "c", "js", "build", "help".

entries.name: setCommand
entries.code: proc setCommand(cmd: string; project = "") {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Sets the Nim command that should be continued with after this Nimscript has finished.

entries.name: cmpic
entries.code: proc cmpic(a, b: string): int {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Compares a and b ignoring case.

entries.name: getEnv
entries.code:
proc getEnv(key: string; default = ""): string {.tags: [ReadIOEffect],
    raises: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Retrieves the environment variable of name key.

entries.name: existsEnv
entries.code:
proc existsEnv(key: string): bool {.tags: [ReadIOEffect], raises: [],
                                    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Checks for the existence of an environment variable named key.

entries.name: putEnv
entries.code: proc putEnv(key, val: string) {.tags: [WriteIOEffect], raises: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Sets the value of the environment variable named key to val.

entries.name: delEnv
entries.description: Deletes the environment variable named key.

entries.name: fileExists
entries.code:
proc fileExists(filename: string): bool {.tags: [ReadIOEffect], raises: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Checks if the file exists.

entries.name: dirExists
entries.code:
proc dirExists(dir: string): bool {.tags: [ReadIOEffect], raises: [],
                                    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Checks if the directory dir exists.

entries.name: selfExe
entries.code:
proc selfExe(): string {.deprecated: "Deprecated since v1.7; Use getCurrentCompilerExe",
                         raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns the currently running nim or nimble executable.

entries.name: toExe
entries.code: proc toExe(filename: string): string {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: On Windows adds ".exe" to filename, else returns filename unmodified.

entries.name: toDll
entries.code: proc toDll(filename: string): string {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: On Windows adds ".dll" to filename, on Posix produces "lib$filename.so".

A shortcut for switch Example:

--path:somePath # same as switch("path", "somePath")
--path:"someOtherPath" # same as switch("path", "someOtherPath")
--hint:"[Conf]:off" # same as switch("hint", "[Conf]:off")

A shortcut for switch Example:

--listCmd # same as switch("listCmd")

entries.name: ScriptMode
entries.code:
ScriptMode {.pure.} = enum
  Silent,                   ## Be silent.
  Verbose,                  ## Be verbose.
  Whatif                     ## Do not run commands, instead just echo what
                             ## would have been done.
entries.description: Controls the behaviour of the script.

entries.name: mode
entries.description: Set this to influence how mkDir, rmDir, rmFile etc. behave

entries.name: listDirs
entries.code:
proc listDirs(dir: string): seq[string] {.raises: [OSError],
    tags: [ReadIOEffect], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Lists all the subdirectories (non-recursively) in the directory dir.

entries.name: listFiles
entries.code:
proc listFiles(dir: string): seq[string] {.raises: [OSError],
    tags: [ReadIOEffect], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Lists all the files (non-recursively) in the directory dir.

entries.name: rmDir
entries.code:
proc rmDir(dir: string; checkDir = false) {.raises: [OSError],
    tags: [ReadIOEffect, WriteIOEffect], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Removes the directory dir.

entries.name: rmFile
entries.code:
proc rmFile(file: string) {.raises: [OSError],
                            tags: [ReadIOEffect, WriteIOEffect], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Removes the file.

entries.name: mkDir
entries.description: Creates the directory dir including all necessary subdirectories. If the directory already exists, no error is raised.

entries.name: mvFile
entries.code:
proc mvFile(from, to: string) {.raises: [OSError],
                                tags: [ReadIOEffect, WriteIOEffect], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Moves the file from to to.

entries.name: mvDir
entries.code:
proc mvDir(from, to: string) {.raises: [OSError],
                               tags: [ReadIOEffect, WriteIOEffect], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Moves the dir from to to.

entries.name: cpFile
entries.code:
proc cpFile(from, to: string) {.raises: [OSError],
                                tags: [ReadIOEffect, WriteIOEffect], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Copies the file from to to.

entries.name: cpDir
entries.code:
proc cpDir(from, to: string) {.raises: [OSError],
                               tags: [ReadIOEffect, WriteIOEffect], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Copies the dir from to to.

entries.name: exec
entries.code:
proc exec(command: string) {.raises: [OSError],
                             tags: [ExecIOEffect, WriteIOEffect], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Executes an external process. If the external process terminates with a non-zero exit code, an OSError exception is raised. The command is executed relative to the current source path.
Note: If you need a version of exec that returns the exit code and text output of the command, you can use system.gorgeEx.

entries.name: exec
entries.code:
proc exec(command: string; input: string; cache = "") {.raises: [OSError],
    tags: [ExecIOEffect, WriteIOEffect], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Executes an external process. If the external process terminates with a non-zero exit code, an OSError exception is raised.
Warning: This version of exec is executed relative to the nimscript module path, which affects how the command resolves relative paths. Thus it is generally better to use gorgeEx directly when you need more control over the execution environment or when working with commands that deal with relative paths.

entries.name: selfExec
entries.description: Executes an external command with the current nim/nimble executable. Command must not contain the "nim " part.

entries.name: put
entries.code: proc put(key, value: string) {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Sets a configuration 'key' like 'gcc.options.always' to its value.

entries.name: get
entries.code: proc get(key: string): string {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Retrieves a configuration 'key' like 'gcc.options.always'.

entries.name: exists
entries.code: proc exists(key: string): bool {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Checks for the existence of a configuration 'key' like 'gcc.options.always'.

entries.name: nimcacheDir
entries.description: Retrieves the location of 'nimcache'.

entries.name: projectName
entries.description: Retrieves the name of the current project

entries.name: projectDir
entries.description: Retrieves the absolute directory of the current project

entries.name: projectPath
entries.description: Retrieves the absolute path of the current project

entries.name: thisDir
entries.description: Retrieves the directory of the current nims script file. Its path is obtained via currentSourcePath (although, currently, currentSourcePath resolves symlinks, unlike thisDir).

entries.name: cd
entries.description:
Changes the current directory.

The change is permanent for the rest of the execution, since this is just a shortcut for os.setCurrentDir() . Use the withDir() template if you want to perform a temporary change only.

entries.name: findExe
entries.code: proc findExe(bin: string): string {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Searches for bin in the current working directory and then in directories listed in the PATH environment variable. Returns "" if the exe cannot be found.

entries.name: withDir
entries.code: template withDir(dir: string; body: untyped): untyped
entries.description:
Changes the current directory temporarily.

If you need a permanent change, use the cd() proc. Usage example:

# inside /some/path/
withDir "foo":
  # move to /some/path/foo/
# back in /some/path/

entries.name: cppDefine
entries.description: tell Nim that define is a C preprocessor #define and so always needs to be mangled.

entries.name: readLineFromStdin
entries.description: Reads a line of data from stdin - blocks until n or EOF which happens when stdin is closed

entries.name: readAllFromStdin
entries.description: Reads all data from stdin - blocks until EOF which happens when stdin is closed

entries.name: task
entries.code: template task(name: untyped; description: string; body: untyped): untyped
entries.description:
Defines a task. Hidden tasks are supported via an empty description.

Example:

task build, "default build is via the C backend":
  setCommand "c"

For a task named foo, this template generates a proc named fooTask. This is useful if you need to call one task in another in your Nimscript.

Example:

task foo, "foo":        # > nim foo
  echo "Running foo"    # Running foo

task bar, "bar":        # > nim bar
  echo "Running bar"    # Running bar
  fooTask()             # Running foo

entries.description: Nimble support: Set this to the package name. It is usually not required to do that, nims' filename is the default.

entries.name: version
entries.description: Nimble support: The package's version.

entries.description: Nimble support: The package's description.

entries.name: license
entries.description: Nimble support: The package's license.

entries.name: srcDir
entries.description: Nimble support: The package's source directory.

entries.name: binDir
entries.description: Nimble support: The package's binary directory.

entries.name: backend

entries.name: requiresData
entries.description: Exposes the list of requirements for read and write accesses.

entries.description: Nimble support: Call this to set the list of requirements of your Nimble package.
